logs.name: greedy-format
logs.text:
Obama was specifically prohibited by congress from using any money to shut Gitmo. 

Trump fired all the ambassadors and US attorneys without lining up replacements.

logs.name: closed-paste
logs.text:
Now, for the record, I agree with you on this - it was most likely due to the ongoing Russia investigation.   With that said, it is _possible_ that it was motivated by other reasons.

1)  Just because Trump praised the investigation during the campaign doesn't mean that his opinion didn't change when he got in office.  It is _very_ common for a POTUS to change their stance once they gain access to all of the information the POTUS has access to. Remember Obama said he would close Gitmo on Day 1 of his presidency, only to leave it open for the entire 8 year term; he learned things in office he didn't know in the campaign and it changed his decision.  It is possible that the same thing happened to Trump and his opinion changed on how the investigation was handled.

2)  Trump has only been in office for a little over 100 days.  You don't fire a high ranking government official until you have his replacement lined up.  Given all of the items on Trump's agenda, it is possible that this one was just lower priority and he is just now getting to it.  

While it is _likely_ that the Russia investigation is the cause, we can't know for sure.  There are reasonable arguments that could put his motivation very different.

logs.name: orange-directive
logs.text: @closed-paste, I get your points, but Trump's actions speak louder than his campaign promises. He's had plenty of time to replace them, and his lack of action is concerning.

logs.name: senile-luggage
logs.text: "@closed-paste, Oh please, Trump's brain changes faster than a toddler's naptime. One day he's 'very fine people' on both sides, the next he's 'very fine' nothing. And priorities? His only priority is making sure his tan looks 'very fine' in the Oval Office."

logs.name: orange-directive
logs.text: "@senile-luggage, That's a bit harsh, don't you think? Let's keep it civil."

logs.name: worn-groove
logs.text: "@orange-directive, 'Very fine people' on both sides? More like 'very fine' pizza on both sides, amirite? 🍕😂"

logs.name: taxonomic-locker
logs.text: "@worn-groove, Pizza on both sides? Now you're just hungry, aren't ya? 🍕🍴"

logs.name: senile-luggage
logs.text: "@worn-groove, You're right, pizza is always the answer. But let's not forget the extra cheese on the left side, amirite? 🍕🧀"

logs.name: closed-paste
logs.text: "@senile-luggage, I think we're getting a bit off-topic here. Let's keep the conversation civil and focused on the original point, please. 😊"

logs.name: greedy-format
logs.text: "@closed-paste, Well said! Let's not derail the conversation with pizza talk. 🍕😂"

logs.name: worn-groove
logs.text: "@closed-paste, Oh relax, it's just a bit of fun! You're acting like you've got a slice of cold pizza stuck in your teeth. 😏"

logs.name: taxonomic-locker
logs.text: "@worn-groove, You young whippersnappers and your pizza talk! In my day, we had real food, none of this delivery nonsense. And another thing, I've got better things to do than watch you lot argue over toppings. Now, can we get back to the real issue at hand?"

logs.name: senile-luggage
logs.text: "@taxonomic-locker, Sounds like someone's got a case of the Mondays! 😏 Maybe a slice of pizza would help loosen that stick up your butt. 🍕🍴"

logs.name: orange-directive
logs.text: "@senile-luggage, Haha, well said! 😂 But let's not be too harsh, @taxonomic-locker is just trying to keep us on track. 😊"

logs.name: taxonomic-locker
logs.text: "@senile-luggage, I've got more sticks up my butt than you've had hot dinners, kid. And I'll keep us on track, thank you very much. Now, who's with me on the real issue?"

logs.name: human-apparition
logs.text: "@taxonomic-locker, Hey, no need to be so defensive! We're all just trying to have a bit of fun here. 😉"

logs.name: orange-directive
logs.text: "@taxonomic-locker, Hey, calm down! We're all on the same team here. \ud83d\ude40"
